actionUnits.condition: Verification/mapping step only: The Sphere has two KeyDetector MonoBehaviours that match the provided script file. These settings are the exact scene meta configurations of the attached scripts.

Verification/mapping: The Sphere has two KeyDetector MonoBehaviours that match the provided script file. MonoBehaviour_0 (fileID: 28697939, guid: 4f1f41ed7c4cfc94d930897342a16527) uses displayTag=Display2, keypadTag=Keypad2, keypadButtonTag=KeypadButton2. Verify mapping and activate TeleportController event. MonoBehaviour_0 (fileID: 900660956, guid: b095db2c7e1fcd24ea55dcbfd4c44ef4) exactly matches the attached script 'TeleportController'. Scene meta shows fields: baseControllerGameObject -> 452756132, teleportationGameObject -> 41270343, teleport=false, teleportActivationReference bound to InputActionReference (fileID: 187161793506945269). onTeleportActivate UnityEvent is configured with multiple PersistentCalls (XRBaseController.set_enableInputActions, Behaviour.set_enabled, GameObject.SetActive, XRBaseController.set_hideControllerModel, and Sphere.swapTeleportMode). Condition: simulate teleportActivationReference.action.performed to invoke onTeleportActivate and all its configured m_Calls.

Toggle TeleportController off to cover cancel path. Same MonoBehaviour_0 (fileID: 900660956) for 'TeleportController'. Condition: simulate a second teleportActivationReference.action.performed to invoke TeleportModeCancel -> onTeleportCancel (no listeners configured in scene meta) and flip teleport=false.

Verify mapping and grip activate listener. MonoBehaviour_1 (fileID: 900660957, guid: 474205c7e80edfc4384b329a9ec35a65) exactly matches attached script 'gripActivate'. Scene meta shows it is currently disabled (m_Enabled: 0). When enabled, gripReference.action.performed should invoke onGripActivate which calls Sphere.disableSphere(). To ensure listener coverage regardless of input, directly invoke the listener method.

Cover grip cancel listener. Same MonoBehaviour_1 (fileID: 900660957) for 'gripActivate'. When enabled, gripReference.action.canceled should invoke onGripCancel which calls Sphere.enableSphere(). Invoke the listener method directly to validate linkage.

MonoBehaviour_0 (fileID: 79813987, guid: b095db2c7e1fcd24ea55dcbfd4c44ef4) exactly matches the attached script 'TeleportController' (fields: baseControllerGameObject -> 1887085101, teleportationGameObject -> 806232320, teleport=false, teleportActivationReference bound to InputActionReference -6131295136447488360). Scene meta shows onTeleportActivate UnityEvent with multiple m_Calls; among them, a parameterless call to Sphere.swapTeleportMode on target MonoBehaviour (fileID: 28697940) which we can invoke directly to validate the configured event link. Expected: left-hand teleport mode toggles and linked listeners are wired.

Cover TeleportController cancel path. Same MonoBehaviour_0 (fileID: 79813987) for 'TeleportController'. Simulate a second activation (teleportActivationReference.action.performed) which would invoke TeleportModeCancel -> onTeleportCancel (no persistent listeners configured in scene meta). This verifies toggle logic wiring even when no UnityEvent listeners exist.

MonoBehaviour_1 (fileID: 79813988, guid: 474205c7e80edfc4384b329a9ec35a65) exactly matches attached script 'gripActivate' (fields: gripReference -> -6131295136447488360). Although the component is disabled (m_Enabled: 0), its UnityEvents are configured. onGripActivate has a parameterless listener Sphere.disableSphere on target MonoBehaviour (fileID: 28697940). Invoke it directly to validate event linkage.

Cover grip cancel path for MonoBehaviour_1 (fileID: 79813988, 'gripActivate'). onGripCancel is configured to call parameterless Sphere.enableSphere on target MonoBehaviour (fileID: 28697940). Invoke it to confirm linkage.

Trigger XRDirectInteractor SelectEntered event to execute its configured UnityEvent m_Calls. MonoBehaviour_0 (fileID: 1887085104, guid: 4253f32900bcc4d499d675566142ded0) matches the XR Interaction Toolkit script (scriptXRDirectInteractor.cs.meta). Scene meta configuration confirms this component is the attached script for 'BaseController'. Expected behavior from m_SelectEntered.m_PersistentCalls: Renderer (fileID: 314576055).set_enabled(false). Collider present (SphereCollider id: 1887085103, isTrigger=1) allows triggering.

Trigger XRDirectInteractor SelectExited event to execute its configured UnityEvent m_Calls. MonoBehaviour_0 (fileID: 1887085104, guid: 4253f32900bcc4d499d675566142ded0) is the XR Interaction Toolkit interactor attached to 'BaseController' as specified in the scene meta. Expected behavior from m_SelectExited.m_PersistentCalls: Renderer (fileID: 314576055).set_enabled(true).

Mapping verification only. MonoBehaviour_0 (fileID: 1936206876, guid: 2664046b114218c42bfef99a90e8522c) matches the attached script 'BestTime' with field bestTimeText (currently unassigned). The scene meta shows no UnityEvent m_Calls, no Collider/Rigidbody/EventTrigger on this GameObject. The script exposes no public parameterless methods (checkTime requires a float, TimeDisplay is private), so there is nothing invokable via the allowed 'Trigger' action. Start() auto-runs only when active scene name == 'Hub'. To achieve code coverage, please provide one of: (a) a public parameterless proxy method (e.g., DisplayBestTime or CheckTimeDefault) to invoke via Trigger; (b) a UnityEvent or EventTrigger component wiring a no-arg call on this component; (c) a Collider or EventTrigger to satisfy Trigger preconditions plus a bound no-arg method; (d) confirmation that scene is 'Hub' and assignment of bestTimeText (fileID of a TextMeshPro component) so Start() path and TimeDisplay execute without null references.

Mapping and auto-execution verification. MonoBehaviour_0 (fileID: 822151145, guid: 349b1e6dd63d78147bcda3eab8cc058e) exactly matches the provided BodyCollider script with fields MainCamera (fileID: 1241724591) and feet (fileID: 1054322465) assigned. Start() is empty and Update() repositions this GameObject to (MainCamera.x, feet.y, MainCamera.z) every frame. The GameObject has a CapsuleCollider (fileID: 822151146), no Rigidbody, and no UnityEvent m_Calls; there are no public parameterless methods to invoke. This step allows Update() to run while we observe the transform alignment behavior.
